docs.title: DGamboa Notes & Snippets
docs.text:
DGamboa Notes & Snippets

Welcome to the "DGamboa Notes & Snippets" repository!

Here, I document my journey through the fascinating world of software development, Python programming, Docker containers, Linux administration, 3D printing, IoT home automation, and various other intriguing technologies.

As I explore these fields, I collect valuable insights, code snippets, and notes to aid my learning process and share with the community. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting your journey, I hope you find something useful here.

Get Involved:

Feel free to explore the contents of this repository, fork it, and contribute back! Your feedback, suggestions, and contributions are highly valued and appreciated.

You can find the full documentation as a static website at DGamboa Notes & Snippets.

Happy coding!

Diego Gamboa

docs.title: Using git sparse-checkout for faster documentation builds
docs.text: Leveraging git sparse-checkout in GitHub Actions enabled us to speed up documentation builds in our repository, cutting checkout times from 20 to 30 seconds to just 2 seconds.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
This page lists the topics that I have written about. Click on a topic to view the notes.

  • Python

    Python is a popular programming language. Here are some notes about Python.

    Notes

  • Docker

    Docker is a platform for developing, shipping, and running applications. Here are some notes about Docker.

    Notes

  • Linux

    Linux bash scripting and command line are powerful tools for automation and system management.

    Notes

  • Self-Hosting

    Self-hosting is a way to take control of your data and services by hosting them yourself. Besides, it is a great way to learn about servers and networking. Here are some notes about self-hosting.

    Notes

  • Home Automation

    In this section, You will find notes about journey into home automation using Home Assistant and ESPHome.

    Notes

  • 3D Printing

    3D printing is an exciting technology that allows you to bring your ideas to life. Here are some notes about 3D printing.

    Notes

docs.title: 3D Printing Notes
docs.text: Explore the exciting world of 3D printing. Discover the basics of 3D printing technology, delve into printer recommendations, and find resources to bring your 3D design ideas to life.

docs.title: Docker Notes
docs.text: Welcome to the world of containerization! Here, we'll dive into Docker, a platform that lets you package your applications with all their dependencies into standardized units called containers. Docker makes deploying and running applications across different environments a breeze. Get ready to learn how to containerize your apps, manage them efficiently, and unlock the power of containerized workflows.

docs.title: Home Automation
docs.text: Get inspired to create a smart home! Find resources on building your own automation projects, hardware recommendations, and DIY tips to bring your home to life.

docs.title: Linux bash scripting and command line notes
docs.text: Unleash the power of your Linux terminal! This section equips you with the magic of Bash scripting and command-line tools. Learn how to automate tasks, navigate your system efficiently, and become a true Linux guru.

docs.title: Python
docs.text: Python is a powerful and versatile programming language that's both beginner-friendly and perfect for complex projects. This blog is your one-stop shop for all things Python! Whether you're a seasoned coder or just starting out, you'll find a treasure trove of tutorials, tips, and tricks to level up your Python skills. Explore the vast capabilities of Python for web development, data science, automation, and more!

docs.title: Arrow
docs.text: Arrow is a Python library that offers a sensible, human-friendly approach to creating, manipulating, formatting, and converting dates and times.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install this library, you can use pip:

installation command
pip install arrow

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
import arrow

utc = arrow.utcnow()
